title: Either Select
description: Define an algebraic data type Either with two constructors: Left(Int) and Right(Int). Write a function that takes three integer parameters (a, b, selector). If selector > 0, construct Left(a); otherwise construct Right(b). Use pattern matching to extract and return the contained integer. Parameters use De Bruijn indexing: @Int.0=selector, @Int.1=b, @Int.2=a.
description_neutral: Given three integers (a, b, selector), if selector > 0 wrap the first value in a Left variant, otherwise wrap the second value in a Right variant. Use pattern matching on the result to extract and return the contained integer.
notes: Parameters: either_select(a, b, selector). @Int.0=selector, @Int.1=b, @Int.2=a. Tests three-parameter De Bruijn indexing combined with conditional ADT construction and matching. Inside Left(@Int) or Right(@Int), @Int.0 refers to the matched payload.
